skill.name: Azure AI Projects SDK for Java
skill.description: Azure AI Projects SDK for Java. High-level SDK for Azure AI Foundry project management including connections, datasets, indexes, and evaluations.
skill.summary: Manage Azure AI Foundry projects with Java SDK for connections, datasets, indexes, and AI model evaluations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill contains documentation-only content for the Azure AI Projects Java SDK. No executable code was scanned. The skill provides usage examples for Azure AI Foundry project management including authentication, client operations, and best practices. Environment variable usage for PROJECT_ENDPOINT is standard practice for Azure SDK configuration.

content.user_title: Manage Azure AI Projects with Java SDK
content.value_statement: Developers need to integrate Azure AI Foundry services into Java applications. This skill provides comprehensive guidance for project management, connections, datasets, and AI model evaluations.
actual_capabilities: Configure Azure AI project authentication using DefaultAzureCredential
List and manage Azure AI resource connections
Create and update search indexes for AI datasets
Access OpenAI evaluation services through the SDK
Handle pagination for listing Azure AI resources
limitations: Requires Azure AI project endpoint and credentials configured via environment variables
Only supports Java applications with Azure SDK dependencies
Does not provide direct AI model inference, only project management capabilities
Beta version SDK may have breaking changes in future releases

use_cases.title: Enterprise AI Integration
use_cases.description: Integrate Azure AI Foundry services into existing Java enterprise applications for unified AI capabilities.
use_cases.target_user: Enterprise Java developers building AI-powered applications

use_cases.title: AI Project Automation
use_cases.description: Automate Azure AI project setup, configuration, and resource management through Java code.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers managing Azure AI infrastructure

use_cases.title: AI Model Evaluation Pipeline
use_cases.description: Build automated evaluation pipelines for AI models using OpenAI evaluation services.
use_cases.target_user: ML engineers validating AI model performance

prompt_templates.title: Basic Project Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to set up Azure AI Projects SDK authentication in Java using environment variables.

prompt_templates.title: List Available Connections
prompt_templates.prompt: Write Java code to list all Azure AI connections and display their names and types.

prompt_templates.title: Create Search Index
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create an Azure AI Search index with a specific connection name and index version.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Evaluation Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to access OpenAI evaluation services through Azure AI Projects SDK and run a model evaluation.

output_examples.input: List all Azure AI connections in the project
output_examples.output: Java code using ConnectionsClient.listConnections() that iterates through PagedIterable and prints each connection name, type, and credential type.
Complete Java example showing AzureAISearchIndex creation with connection name and index name configuration, including error handling.
Try-catch block demonstrating ResourceNotFoundException and HttpResponseException handling with appropriate error messages.

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for production authentication instead of hardcoded credentials
Reuse the AIProjectClientBuilder to create multiple sub-clients efficiently
Store project endpoint and connection names in environment variables for security
anti_patterns: Hardcoding credentials or endpoints directly in source code
Creating new client builders for each operation instead of reusing
Ignoring pagination when listing large collections of resources

faq.question: What Azure SDK dependencies do I need?
faq.answer: Add azure-ai-projects (com.azure:azure-ai-projects) and azure-identity (com.azure:azure-identity) to your Maven or Gradle build file.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure AI Foundry?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which supports multiple authentication methods including managed identity, environment credentials, and Azure CLI.

faq.question: What is the PROJECT_ENDPOINT format?
faq.answer: The endpoint follows the pattern: https://<resource>.services.ai.azure.com/api/projects/<project> where resource and project are your Azure AI resource names.

faq.question: Can I use this SDK for AI model inference?
faq.answer: This SDK focuses on project management. Use Azure OpenAI SDK or AI Model Inference SDK for actual model inference operations.

faq.question: Is this SDK stable for production use?
faq.answer: The SDK is currently in beta (1.0.0-beta.1). Review the changelog before upgrading and test thoroughly in non-production environments.

faq.question: How do I handle large result sets?
faq.answer: Use the PagedIterable response type which handles pagination automatically. Iterate through results and the SDK fetches additional pages as needed.
